---
title: "Linear model"
subtitle: "Fit voxel-wise GLMs from a design matrix."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Build a Design

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": np.arange(0, 96, 8),
    "condition": ["face", "scene", "tool"] * 4,
})

model = fm.event_model(
    'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")',
    data=events,
    sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=120, TR=1.0),
)

X = np.asarray(model.design_matrix)
X.shape
```

## Simulate Voxel Data

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(5)
n_voxels = 6
true_beta = np.array([
    np.linspace(0.8, 1.2, n_voxels),
    np.linspace(-0.4, 0.2, n_voxels),
    np.linspace(0.1, 0.5, n_voxels),
])

Y = X @ true_beta + rng.normal(scale=0.08, size=(X.shape[0], n_voxels))
Y.shape
```

## Fit OLS

`fmri_ols_fit()` is a compact matrix-level helper. Rows are time points and
columns are voxels.

```{python}
fit = fm.fmri_ols_fit(Y, X)

fit["beta"].shape, fit["se"].shape, fit["t"].shape
```

```{python}
beta = pd.DataFrame(fit["beta"], index=model.column_names)
beta.round(3)
```

## Compute a Contrast

```{python}
columns = model.column_names
face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns))
face_vs_scene[columns.index("condition_condition.face")] = 1
face_vs_scene[columns.index("condition_condition.scene")] = -1

contrast_beta = face_vs_scene @ fit["beta"]
contrast_beta.round(3)
```

## Voxel-Wise Covariates

The compatibility helper can append one voxel-specific nuisance column per
voxel.

```{python}
voxelwise_motion = rng.normal(size=Y.shape)
fit_with_voxel_cov = fm.fmri_ols_fit(
    Y,
    X,
    voxelwise=voxelwise_motion,
    voxel_name="motion_proxy",
)

fit_with_voxel_cov["beta"].tail(1)
```

## Next

- [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
- [Sketched and AR-aware models](sketched-ar-models.qmd)
